_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA state legislators batch (state tier). Northern Virginia Senate D-37/38/39/40 + HD-1. Salim, Boysko, Bennett-Parker, Favola, Hope. Evidence-based finalization from primary roll-call/sponsorship records (LIS, VPAP, ChoiceTracker, ReproRising, Giffords) + official roster pages. All five are uniformly progressive Democrats with documented contrary-to-rubric records; no cross-party deviations found. Cells with only caucus-bloc inference (no named roll call / specific sponsorship) left NULL per the cardinal rule. ROSTER NUANCE: Elizabeth Bennett-Parker won a Feb 10, 2026 special election to Senate District 39 (sworn Feb 18, 2026) succeeding Adam Ebbin; scorecard office is now correct, but most of her record is from her prior House of Delegates HD-5 seat.

profile.religion: Muslim (Islam) — first publicly Muslim member of the Virginia Senate
profile.background: VA State Senator, District 37 (Fairfax/Falls Church/Vienna/Tysons/Oakton), in office since Jan 2024. Born Noakhali, Bangladesh (1990), immigrated age 10, raised in Falls Church. George Mason BA + MPA; career in financial services.

saddam-azlan-salim.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-37 (Fairfax/Falls Church), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record and a LEAD author of the marquee items this rubric opposes: chief patron of SJ247, the 2025 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (voted YES; Planned Parenthood + REPRO Rising endorsed; 'protect and expand access to abortion'); co-patron of SJ249, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (voted YES); chief patron of the assault-weapons + large-capacity-magazine ban SB749 (Giffords-endorsed); chief patron of SB351/352/783 restricting state-local 287(g) ICE cooperation ('Virginia will not be a staging ground for an unconstitutional mass deportation campaign'); sponsored DMV automatic-voter-registration. No conservative deviations found. christian_liberty, refuse_federal_overreach, and several sub-cells (embryonic research, euthanasia, CBDC, E-Verify, constitutional carry) returned NO public evidence and are left null.

0.note: Chief patron of SJ247, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; voted YES; policy page: 'protect and expand access to abortion.' Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Sponsored expansion of abortion access — the opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood and REPRO Rising Virginia.

0.note: Co-patron of SJ249, the constitutional amendment to repeal Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban; voted YES (Senate 24-15, Jan 2025).

1.note: Voted YES to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Sponsored DMV automatic-voter-registration update; supports expanded ballot access rather than single-day in-person voting.

1.note: Chief patron of SB351/352/783 restricting state cooperation with federal deportation; opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Authored bills limiting 287(g) ICE cooperation — pro-sanctuary posture, opposite of federal-preemption against sanctuary.

1.note: Chief patron of SB749 (assault-weapons + large-capacity-magazine ban), SB848 (raise purchase age), SB891 (waiting period); supports red-flag laws; Giffords-endorsed.

profile.background: VA State Senator, District 38 (Fairfax/Herndon/Reston), since 2019; prior House of Delegates 2015/16-2019. Hollins University BA; community organizer.

jennifer-boysko.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-38 (Fairfax/Herndon/Reston), since 2019. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Chief patron of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247/2025) AND chief patron of SB280, the medical aid-in-dying (physician-assisted suicide) bill — a documented FALSE on the euthanasia cell. Voted to advance HJ3 (same-sex-marriage repeal) with a floor speech in favor. Voted for HB44 consent/inclusive sex-ed curriculum. Pro-immigrant: chief patron of in-state-tuition-regardless-of-status (SB935) and ICE-detention-oversight (SB5017). Giffords-endorsed gun-control record (2020 package: universal background checks, red-flag, handgun limits; chief patron of a 2023 safe-storage bill). No cross-party deviations. NARAL/EMILY's List endorsement relationship documented; exact donor dollars not pulled. Sub-cells with no public record left null.

0.note: Chief patron of SJ247, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (abortion/contraception/IVF). Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Lead sponsor of abortion-rights expansion — opposite of abortion-abolition.

3.note: Chief patron of SB280, the medical aid-in-dying (physician-assisted suicide) bill; passed Senate 21-19 — does not oppose physician-assisted suicide.

4.note: Endorsed/rated by NARAL Pro-Choice Virginia and EMILY's List.

0.note: Voted to advance HJ3 (repeal of the same-sex-marriage ban) and spoke on the floor in favor ('a moment in history to right a wrong').

1.note: Supported codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Voted for HB44 (consent education in sex-ed) and patroned a 'more inclusive' history/social-studies curriculum — supports comprehensive sex-ed/inclusive curricula.
Supports same-day voter registration and expanded ballot access (chief patron HB272 registration-error notice); opposite of single-day in-person restriction.

1.note: Chief patron of in-state tuition regardless of immigration status (SB935) and co-led driver-privilege cards for undocumented immigrants — opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Chief patron of SB5017 (state oversight of ICE detention facilities); pro-immigrant/anti-enforcement posture, opposite of federal-preemption against sanctuary.

1.note: Giffords-endorsed; co-patroned the 2020 gun package (universal background checks, red-flag/extreme-risk, handgun purchase limits) and chief-patroned a 2023 safe-storage bill.

set.office: State Senate — District 39 (special-election winner, sworn Feb 18, 2026; prior House of Delegates HD-5, 2022-2026)

profile.background: VA State Senator, District 39 (Alexandria/Arlington), since Feb 18, 2026 (won special election Feb 10, 2026 succeeding Adam Ebbin); prior House of Delegates HD-5, 2022-2026; Alexandria City Council/Vice Mayor 2019-2021. Cornell BA, Univ. of London MA; nonprofit executive (founded a food-waste social enterprise).

elizabeth-bennett-parker.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-39 (Alexandria/Arlington), sworn Feb 18, 2026 after a special election; nearly her entire voting record is from her prior House of Delegates HD-5 seat (2022-2026). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Voted YEA on SJ247 (reproductive-freedom amendment; Planned Parenthood Advocates VA 2025 endorsee) and co-sponsored HJ9 (same-sex-marriage repeal, House 58-35); publicly opposed the Youngkin administration's transgender-youth school policies (parental-notification model). Chief patron of HJ2, the felon-voting-restoration constitutional amendment. Pro-sanctuary: backs banning 287(g), restricting ICE in schools/hospitals. Gun-control author: HB93/HB46 (disarming domestic abusers), co-patron of the assault-weapons ban HB217; Giffords-endorsed. No cross-party deviations. Election sub-cells (ID/mail-in machinery) and several others left null where only felon-restoration/anti-purge measures exist, which don't map cleanly to the ballot-mechanics cells.

0.note: Voted YEA on SJ247 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment); Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia 2025 endorsee. Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Champions abortion-access expansion — opposite of abortion-abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia (2025).

0.note: Co-sponsored HJ9, the constitutional amendment repealing the same-sex-marriage ban (House passed 58-35, Jan 2025).

1.note: Supported codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Publicly opposed the Youngkin administration's transgender-youth school policies; affirms LGBTQ identity rather than biological-sex framing.

4.note: Opposed parental-notification trans-youth school policy; supports LGBTQ-affirming policy in schools.

1.note: Opposed the Youngkin parental-notification model policy on transgender students — opposite of parental notification/consent on minors' gender decisions.
Supports restricting immigration enforcement and barring police from assisting ICE without a judicial warrant; opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Supports banning 287(g) agreements and terminating existing ones — pro-sanctuary, opposite of federal-preemption against sanctuary.

1.note: Authored HB93/HB46 (disarming domestic abusers); co-patron of the assault-weapons/large-capacity-magazine ban HB217; Giffords-endorsed.

profile.background: VA State Senator, District 40 (Arlington), since 2012 (SD-31 2012-2024, SD-40 2024-present); Arlington County Board 1997-2012. 15-year policy advisor at U.S. HHS. Chairs Senate Education & Health and the Women's Health Care Caucus.

barbara-favola.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-40 (Arlington), since 2012. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record; 'Pro-Choice Champion' (NARAL/Planned Parenthood/EMILY's List/REPRO endorsed). Co-patron of SJ247 (reproductive-freedom amendment); chief patron of SB733 (repealed the 24-hr wait/ultrasound/counseling) and SB743 (abortion-extradition shield). On her own access-to-voting page she claims credit for the Democratic majority that eliminated the photo-ID requirement and added no-excuse absentee, drop boxes, and same-day registration. Criminal-justice-reform sponsor (banned no-knock warrants, pretext stops; sentencing reform, expungement). Anti-enforcement on immigration (opposed sanctuary-city bans and locality status-check mandates). Gun-control sponsor (HB93/SB38 firearm removal under protective orders; red-flag expansion). NO cross-party deviations. NOTE: her individual NAMED roll call on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ3) was not isolated in sources (caucus-bloc 26-13 only) — biblical_marriage left NULL per the cardinal rule rather than inferred from party.

0.note: Co-patron of SJ247; chief patron of SB733 (repealed 24-hr wait/ultrasound/counseling); rated 'Pro-Choice Champion.' Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Long pro-abortion-access sponsorship record — opposite of abortion-abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by NARAL Pro-Choice Virginia, Planned Parenthood, and EMILY's List.

1.note: Floor advocacy to protect a minor's judicial bypass of parental consent on abortion — opposite of parental consent on minors' medical decisions.
Part of the majority that eliminated Virginia's photo-ID requirement (claims credit on her own access-to-voting page).

2.note: Added no-excuse absentee and same-day registration — opposite of single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Added ballot drop boxes — opposite of opposing mass mail-in/drop boxes.

1.note: Sponsored banning no-knock warrants and pretext stops, sentencing reform, expungement, and marijuana decriminalization — criminal-justice-leniency direction.
Aligned with the bloc that killed GOP immigration-enforcement bills; backed FAMIS prenatal funding for immigrant mothers — opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Opposed bills banning sanctuary cities and locality immigration-status-check mandates — opposite of federal-preemption against sanctuary.

1.note: Co-sponsored HB93/SB38 (firearm removal under protective orders) and supports red-flag expansion and an assault-weapons ban.

profile.religion: No public religious affiliation found (degrees from Catholic-affiliated institutions, but no public statement of personal faith)
profile.background: VA Delegate, District 1 (Arlington), continuously since Jan 2010 (renumbered to District 1 after the 2024 redistricting); re-elected Nov 2025. Healthcare attorney; chairs House Courts of Justice, Public Safety, and Health & Human Resources committees. St. Mary's BA, Catholic University MA + JD.

patrick-hope.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-1 (Arlington), since 2010. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Co-patron of HJ1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; 'Pro-Choice Champion'; Planned Parenthood/NARAL/NOW endorsed) and HJ9 (same-sex-marriage amendment, House 58-35). Co-patron of HJ2 (felon-voting-restoration amendment). As Public Safety Committee chair, championed universal background checks, red-flag laws, an assault-weapons ban, and lost/stolen-firearm reporting mandates; Giffords-endorsed. No cross-party deviations found. Dimensions family_child_sovereignty, christian_liberty, refuse_federal_overreach, and border_immigration returned NO public record for him individually and are left null per the cardinal rule (do not infer from party).

0.note: Co-patron of HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; rated 'Pro-Choice Champion.' Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Consistent pro-abortion-access voting record — opposite of abortion-abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood, NARAL Pro-Choice Virginia, and NOW VA.

0.note: Co-patron of HJ9, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (House passed 58-35, Jan 2025).

1.note: Supported codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.
As Public Safety Committee chair, championed universal background checks, red-flag/extreme-risk orders, an assault-weapons ban, and lost/stolen-firearm reporting; Giffords-endorsed.
